paper.title: Acoustic emission analysis for the quantification of autonomous crack healing in concrete
paper.authors: Kim Van Tittelboom; Nele De Belie; Frank Lehmann; Christian U. Grosse
paper.material_system: self-healing concrete beams with encapsulated healing agents

evidence.evidence_text: The amount of strength regain, calculated according to Eq. (1),i s shown in Fig. 6. When considering the results shown in Fig. 6, a sig- niﬁcant difference can be noticed between the amount of strength regain obtained for untreated (‘REF’) and treated (‘EPO’, ‘PUR’, ‘SHC_PUR’ and ‘PURO’) cracks. While for the reference specimens the amount of strength regain is lower than 0%, the other test series show an average regain in strength varying from 35 till 80% of the original strength. Furthermore, a signiﬁcant difference is noticed be- tween the series ‘PUR’ and ‘PURO’ at the one hand and ‘EPO’ and ‘SHC_PUR’ at the other hand. It seems that manual healing of cracks by means of polyurethane is less effective than self-healing by using the same type of healing agent. As the same amount of strength re- gain is noticed for t
